name: Àger - Pedra - Colobor Loop
name_original: Running - Circular por el Valle de Ager
description: This difficult 9.1-mile (14.7 km) jogging loop in Montsec d'Ares offers panoramic views and passes the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Stone.
region_intro: Jogging around Àger offers diverse natural landscapes, primarily within the scenic Àger Valley. The region is characterized by its dramatic geological formations, including the prominent Mont-rebei Gorge and the unique "Chinese Wall of Finestres" (Roques de la Vila). Running trails in Àger often feature varied terrain, from valley paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes. This area provides a natural setting for outdoor activities, including running and hiking. Best jogging routes around Àger The most popular jogging route is Àger - Pedra - Colobor Loop, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging experience through the Àger Valley. Another top favourite among local runners is Refugio de Colobor – Refugio de Colobor loop from Àger, a difficult 8.8 miles (14.2 km) path. This trail features varied terrain around the Colobor refuge, providing a demanding run. Local runners also love the Mont-rebei Gorge – Cami Vell de Mont-rebei loop from Aparcament de la Pertusa 1, a 21.6 miles (34.8 km) trail leading through the dramatic Mont-rebei Gorge, often completed in about 5 hours 29 minutes. Jogging around Àger is defined by the scenic Àger Valley, the impressive Mont-rebei Gorge, and the striking Roques de la Vila. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult trails. The routes in Àger are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Àger's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Àger?
A: Àger offers a diverse selection of 18 running routes. While many are challenging, there are options that cater to different fitness levels, allowing you to explore the stunning Àger Valley.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Àger?
A: The running trails in Àger feature a varied and dramatic landscape. You'll encounter paths through the scenic Àger Valley, as well as more challenging routes with significant elevation changes, particularly around the impressive Mont-rebei Gorge. The region's geological formations provide a refreshing change from urban running surfaces.

Q: Are there any easy running routes in Àger for beginners?
A: While Àger is known for its more challenging trails, there are 5 routes classified as moderate, which can be suitable for runners looking for a less intense experience than the difficult options. These routes still offer the beautiful natural setting of the Àger Valley.

Q: What are some of the most scenic running routes in Àger?
A: For breathtaking views, consider routes that traverse the Mont-rebei Gorge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24317677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-rebei Gorge – Cami Vell de Mont-rebei loop from Aparcament de la Pertusa 1&lt;/a&gt; offers dramatic scenery, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Mont-rebei Gorge loop from Serra del Mont-sec&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive vistas of this natural wonder.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Àger?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Àger are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931206461/ager-pedra-colobor-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Àger - Pedra - Colobor Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10467980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio de Colobor – Refugio de Colobor loop from Àger&lt;/a&gt;, both offering a comprehensive experience of the area.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the running routes?
A: Àger's routes often pass by fascinating natural and historical sites. You might encounter the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2670454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinese Wall of Finestres&lt;/a&gt;, a striking geological formation, or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova del Tabac&lt;/a&gt;, a monumental cave. The region also features historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Sant Pere&lt;/a&gt; and various castles.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Àger?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running experience in Àger, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners frequently praise the diverse natural landscapes, the challenging yet rewarding terrain, and the stunning views, especially around the Mont-rebei Gorge, which make for a memorable outdoor adventure.

Q: Is Àger a good destination for trail running?
A: Absolutely. With its varied terrain, significant elevation changes, and dramatic natural features like the Mont-rebei Gorge and the Àger Valley, Àger is an excellent destination for trail running enthusiasts seeking both challenging and visually rewarding routes.

Q: What is the best season for running in Àger?
A: The Àger Valley offers a vibrant outdoor environment throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for running. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are advisable, especially on routes with less shade.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in Àger?
A: Yes, Àger caters to long-distance runners with several extensive routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24317677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-rebei Gorge – Cami Vell de Mont-rebei loop from Aparcament de la Pertusa 1&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 21.6-mile (34.8 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10468124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Village of Rúbies – Portella Blanca loop from Font de les Bagasses&lt;/a&gt; covers 20.9 miles (33.6 km), providing demanding runs through the region's impressive landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that combine natural beauty with historical sites?
A: Many routes in Àger offer a blend of natural beauty and historical interest. You can explore trails that wind through the stunning Àger Valley and also pass by ancient structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1127644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Sant Oïsme&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6762070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Viacamp and Hermitage of San Esteban&lt;/a&gt;, enriching your running experience with cultural insights.
tips: The name of this hermitage "Mother of God of Stone" leaves no doubt about the rocky nature of the environment that surrounds it. And it is that this hermitage has been built on bare stone, integrating perfectly into it.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Àger - Pedra - Colobor Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this loop is quite demanding, characterized by stony and rocky paths with frequent loose stones, especially on ascents and descents. You'll traverse rocky sections, mountain oak, and pine forests. While some parts might be wide and uncomplicated, expect a generally rugged and uneven surface.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for jogging or running?
faqs.answer: Despite being listed as a jogging route, the Àger - Pedra - Colobor Loop is generally not recommended for continuous jogging. It features strong ascents and descents over stony paths with loose stones, making it easy to slip. It's better suited for experienced hikers with good physical fitness and appropriate footwear.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Àger - Pedra - Colobor Loop?
faqs.answer: For hikers, this challenging loop is estimated to take around 4 hours and 30 minutes to complete. This duration accounts for the significant elevation changes and difficult terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Àger - Pedra - Colobor Loop?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details are not provided, but the trail typically starts and ends near Àger. It's advisable to check local information or maps for designated parking areas close to the main trailhead in Àger before your visit.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers magnificent panoramic views of the valley and a spectacular landscape of Mediterranean forests. You'll pass by historical sites such as the 11th and 16th-century Hermitage of the Virgin of the Stone and the 16th-century sanctuary-refuge of Colobor. Keep an eye out for large birds of prey overhead.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to experience this trail?
faqs.answer: To avoid extreme heat and enjoy more comfortable hiking conditions, the best times to visit are typically spring and autumn. Summers can be very hot, and many sections lack shade, making it less enjoyable. Winters might present colder temperatures and potentially icy conditions on higher, shaded parts of the trail.

faqs.question: Is the Àger - Pedra - Colobor Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, trails in the Montsec d'Ares region generally allow dogs. However, due to the challenging, rocky terrain and potential for loose stones, it's crucial that your dog is fit and accustomed to such conditions. Always keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Montsec d'Ares area?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Montsec d'Ares area, including the Àger - Pedra - Colobor Loop. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning nature conservation and private property.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other official trails. Notably, it covers significant portions of the Etapa 05, Àger - Font del Grabieló, Montsec-Colobor, and Variant Volta Sant Alís trails. You may also briefly encounter parts of the Ametlla and Carxeu routes.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and terrain, you should bring sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a map or GPS device. Trekking poles are highly recommended for stability on the steep, stony ascents and descents.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for this loop?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for a sustained effort, especially on the return journey along the 'camino de los Petrolers,' which is noted as long. The steep sections with loose stones require careful footing. Starting early in the day is advisable, particularly in warmer months, to avoid the midday heat and allow ample time to complete the loop safely.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of the Stone
